There is not enough information available about Elaphrium acuminatum. However, I can provide information on Elaphrium paniculatum. If that is the plant you are referring to, here is a paragraph about it:

 Elaphrium paniculatum, commonly known as Elaphrium, is a plant species that belongs to the family Fabaceae. Elaphrium is used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antidiarrheal properties. The bioactive constituents of Elaphrium include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Elaphrium is often prepared as a decoction or infusion to be used in traditional herbal medicine, and it may also be found in some modern herbal supplements.
